name: Bulgur Bread #2
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the bulgur and salt in a food processor.
2. Pour the boiling water over the bulgur and salt, then stir or process briefly to mix.
3. Let stand for thirty minutes.
4. When ready, add 1 cup flour and the minced onion, and process for 1 minute.
5. The dough should come together into a large ball as you process. If it doesn't, feel the dough: if it feels dry and floury, start the processor, add 2 tablespoons warm water and process for 30 seconds; again if the bulgur and flour still don't come together, add another 2 tablespoons of water and process.
6. Once a ball of dough forms, process for 2 to 3 minutes longer.
7. Turn out onto a well-floured surface. If your processor is too small to handle the whole dough, work with half at a time, adding 1/2 cup flour to each portion, then combine when you turn out and knead by hand.
8. Knead by hand for 4 to 5 minutes. Alternatively, if you do not have a food processor, combine bulgur, salt, and boiling water in a medium-sized bowl and let stand for 30 minutes.
9. Add onions and 1/2 cup flour, then stir in with your hands.
10. Add another 1/2 cup flour and fold into the dough using your hands. With persistence, you will have a dough that is smooth and workable; it may be sticky at first but will soon come together.
11. Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let stand until ready to proceed further, for 15 minutes to an hour.
12. Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C) with baking stones or quarry tiles in place on a rack in the bottom third of the oven.
13. Divide the dough into 8 pieces and flatten each on a well-floured bread board.
14. Roll out one or two loaves until very thin, about 8-10 inches in diameter.
15. Work with only a few at a time, as many as will fit in your oven.
16. When ready, handle the bread gently and place it on the hot tiles.
17. Bake for 1 1/2 to 2 minutes on one side, then turn over and bake for another 1-1 1/2 minutes on the other side until browned around the edges.
18. Keep warm by stacking and wrapping in a clean kitchen towel.
19. Serve warm. Yield: 8 thin, supple flatbreads between 8 and 10 inches in diameter.
